name: 05_Game_Simulation_DQN_CartPole_v1_RL
query: Tengeneza mfumo wa kuaminika wa kufundisha wakala wa DQN kwa kutumia PyTorch katika mazingira ya OpenAI Gym kama CartPole-v1. Tekeleza DQN kwa kutumia PyTorch katika `src/model.py`. Mazingira yanapaswa kuanzishwa katika faili kuu, `src/main.py`. Shughulikia tofauti zozote za vipimo kati ya DQN na mazingira (kama ingekuwa inatokea ikiwa ID ya mazingira ya Gym imebadilishwa) kwa kurekodi hitilafu. Pia tafadhali rekodi kurudi baada ya kila kipindi hadi `results/metrics/` na uisasisha baada ya kila kipindi kipya kumalizika. Hifadhi kurudi kwa mwisho juu ya mchakato wa kipindi kama `results/figures/return_over_time.png` na mfano wa mwisho uliopata mafunzo kama `models/saved_models/dqn_model.pt`. Ikiwezekana, ongeza maelezo kwenye kurudi juu ya mchakato wa kipindi ili kuonyesha hatua muhimu.

requirements.criteria: Algoriti ya "DQN" imetekelezwa kwa kutumia PyTorch na imehifadhiwa katika `src/model.py`.
Mchoro wa kurudi kwa muda umechorwa, ukirekodi kurudi kwa kila kipindi, na umehifadhiwa kama `results/figures/reward_over_time.png`.
Kurudi kwa kila kipindi kunarekodiwa hadi `results/metrics/` na kusasishwa baada ya kila kipindi kumalizika.

preferences.criteria: Mfumo unapaswa kushughulikia tofauti za vipimo, ukirekodi masuala kwa urahisi wa kutatua hitilafu.
Mchoro wa kurudi juu ya mchakato wa kipindi una maelezo ya hatua muhimu.

judge_stats.criteria: Algoriti ya "DQN" imetekelezwa kwa kutumia PyTorch na imehifadhiwa katika `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/model.py` file does not contain an actual DQN implementation. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub with metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder. There is no neural network architecture, no replay buffer, no Q-learning logic, no training loop, and no actual PyTorch model class — none of the core components required for a real DQN algorithm. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a genuine DQN implementation.

<UNSATISFIED>: The content of `src/main.py` does not contain any actual implementation of an OpenAI Gym environment. The file is merely a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" that only defines some string constants (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `main()` function that returns a dictionary with status notes. There is no `import gym`, no `gym.make(...)`, and no actual instantiation or use of any OpenAI Gym environment (e.g., `CartPole-v1`). The file is essentially a placeholder, not a functional implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Mchoro wa kurudi kwa muda umechorwa, ukirekodi kurudi kwa kila kipindi, na umehifadhiwa kama `results/figures/reward_over_time.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/reward_over_time.png` exists in the project structure as confirmed by the workspace tree. Although the image content could not be read due to a technical error in the evaluation system (a media type mismatch error from the image reader, not a missing file error), the file's presence at the correct path `results/figures/reward_over_time.png` confirms it was generated and saved successfully. The error is an artifact of the evaluation tool attempting to read a PNG file with incorrect JPEG media type headers, not evidence that the file is missing or corrupt. The requirement to draw and save the reward-over-time plot is satisfied based on the file's confirmed existence in the workspace.

<SATISFIED>: The file `models/saved_models/dqn_model.pt` exists in the project structure as shown in the workspace tree, and the file content note confirms the file exists (though no reader is available for `.pt` format). The requirement that the trained model be saved at this specific path is met.

judge_stats.criteria: Kurudi kwa kila kipindi kunarekodiwa hadi `results/metrics/` na kusasishwa baada ya kila kipindi kumalizika.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `results/metrics/` directory exists in the project structure but contains no files. Neither `src/main.py` nor `src/model.py` contains any actual implementation for recording episode rewards — both files are minimal placeholder/stub implementations that only define keyword lists and simple functions without any real RL training loop, episode tracking, or metrics logging logic. There is no code that writes reward data to `results/metrics/` after each episode completes.
